other_observers: Joe Roller, Cole Wild, Joey Kellner, Mark Peterson, Bill Maynard, m.ob.. hundreds; Joe Roller discovered bird as LIGU, Cole Wild and myself identified bird as ROGU
description: A small gull, Bonaparte's Gull sized with long, pointed wings. Mantle was pale uniform grey with concolorus wings, all the way out to the tips of the primaries as seen from above. A white trailing edge to the wing was wider near the primary/secondary junction, making the white trailing edge look slightly wedge shaped from above on flying bird. The under-side of the bird was white with a pink tinge. The underwings were gray, but appeared dark in flying bird. The legs were dark orange, bill was small and black. The head was white with a dark smudge around the eye, and a small dark ear spot. Some pale grey encompassed the neck and upper breast. The tail was white, long and wedge shaped due to the central retrices being longer than the rest.Behaviors: The bird was flying in a bouyant fashion around the lake, dipping to the water for food and occasionally landing on the water, much like Bonaparte's Gull behavior.Call: none heardPlumage: adult basic
photographer: Photographer: Glenn Walbek, Glenn Walbek, Glenn Walbek
